title: Khijadiya Bird Sanctuary
tagline: Khijadiya Bird Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Gujarat

overview.short: Khijadiya Bird Sanctuary is a bird sanctuary located in Jamnagar district of Gujarat, India.
overview.description: Khijadiya Bird Sanctuary is a bird sanctuary located in Jamnagar district of Gujarat, India. About 300 species of migratory birds have been recorded here. In 2022, on World Wetlands Day (2 February) it was declared as a Ramsar site.

topPlaces.name: Rangmati River
topPlaces.description: Rangmati River is a river in western India in Gujarat whose origin is near Rampar. Its basin has a maximum length of 50 km.

topPlaces.name: Navagam Ghed
topPlaces.description: Navagam Ghed is a city and a municipality in Jamnagar district in the Indian state of Gujarat.

topPlaces.name: Jambuda
topPlaces.description: Jambuda is a small village in Jamnagar district, which is a part of the Indian state of Gujarat. It is situated 16 kilometres (9.9 mi) from Jamnagar towards Rajkot on State Highway.

topPlaces.name: Nawanagar State
topPlaces.description: Nawanagar (present day Jamnagar) was an Indian state and then a princely state in the historical Halar region, located on the southern shores of the Gulf of Kutch. It was ruled by the Jadeja Rajput dynasty and became a part of newly formed India.

topPlaces.name: Jamnagar
topPlaces.description: Jamnagar (Gujarati: Jāmnagar, pronounced [dʒaːmᵊn̪əgəɾᵊ] ) is a city and the headquarters of Jamnagar district in the Indian state of Gujarat. The city lies just to the south of the Gulf of Kutch, some 337 kilometres (209 mi) west of the state capital, Gandhinagar.

topPlaces.name: Navanagar
topPlaces.description: Navanagar is the new planned city created because of submergence of parts of old Bagalkot city, Karnataka, India. The blueprint for the township was prepared by Charles Correa.

topPlaces.name: Lakhota Lake
topPlaces.description: Lakhota Lake is a man-made lake situated in centre of Jamnagar in the Indian state of Gujarat. The lake is popularly known as Lakhota Talav or Ranmal Lake.

topPlaces.name: Bedi, Gujarat
topPlaces.description: Bedi is a census town and port in Jamnagar Taluka of Jamnagar district, Gujarat, India.

items.note: Rangmati River is a river in western India in Gujarat whose origin is near Rampar. Its basin has a maximum length of 50 km.
Navagam Ghed is a city and a municipality in Jamnagar district in the Indian state of Gujarat.
Jambuda is a small village in Jamnagar district, which is a part of the Indian state of Gujarat. It is situated 16 kilometres (9.9 mi) from Jamnagar towards Rajkot on State Highway.

Nawanagar (present day Jamnagar) was an Indian state and then a princely state in the historical Halar region, located on the southern shores of the Gulf of Kutch. It was ruled by the Jadeja Rajput dynasty and became a part of newly formed India.

Jamnagar (Gujarati: Jāmnagar, pronounced [dʒaːmᵊn̪əgəɾᵊ] ) is a city and the headquarters of Jamnagar district in the Indian state of Gujarat. The city lies just to the south of the Gulf of Kutch, some 337 kilometres (209 mi) west of the state capital, Gandhinagar.
Navanagar is the new planned city created because of submergence of parts of old Bagalkot city, Karnataka, India. The blueprint for the township was prepared by Charles Correa.
Lakhota Lake is a man-made lake situated in centre of Jamnagar in the Indian state of Gujarat. The lake is popularly known as Lakhota Talav or Ranmal Lake.
Bedi is a census town and port in Jamnagar Taluka of Jamnagar district, Gujarat, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Khijadiya Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Khijadiya Bird Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Khijadiya Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jamnagar Airport (14 km) and the nearest railway station is Rajkot Junction (71 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Khijadiya Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Khijadiya Bird Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Khijadiya Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Khijadiya Bird Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Khijadiya Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Rangmati River, Navagam Ghed, Jambuda, Nawanagar State, Jamnagar.
